$comment: TF-C51 (D-FF1-STATICLIB-FAT-ARCHIVE): the FAT/merged static-library RUNTIME witness at the corpus tier. Building a `-staticlib` while passing INPUT `--resolve-library` static archives MERGES their members into the output library. This example needs a NESTED dependsOn (D-EXAMPLES-RUNNER-MULTI-ARTIFACT extension): each target's dependsOn is the FAT lib `fatlib.a` (from fatlib.c, defining dss_fat_extra), whose OWN nested dependsOn is the INPUT lib `input.a` (from input.c, defining dss_input_answer==42). The runner builds bottom-up: input.a, then fatlib.a RESOLVING input.a (the FAT MERGE -> fatlib.a carries BOTH members), then main RESOLVING ONLY fatlib.a. main references dss_input_answer, reaching it SOLELY via the merged copy in fatlib.a (input.a is NOT on main's --resolve-library) -> runs -> exit 42 IFF the merge worked. RED-ON-DISABLE (both gates verified, x86_64 native + arm64 qemu): (1) BUILD -- removing the merge restores the D_StaticLibFatArchiveUnsupported fail-loud so fatlib.a's dependsOn build returns rc!=0 -> red; (2) RUN -- a silent-no-op merge leaves fatlib.a without dss_input_answer and main STILL links (the open D-LINK-EXEC-UNDEFINED-SYMBOL-FAIL-LOUD gap: DSS emits the exec with the symbol unresolved) but fails at RUNTIME with the loader's 'undefined symbol: dss_input_answer' -> exit 127 != 42 -> the runner's exit-code assert fires -> red. NOTE: artifact names track the driver's `<sourceStem>.a`/`.lib`/`.exe` output naming (input.c -> input.a|input.lib, fatlib.c -> fatlib.a|fatlib.lib, main.c -> main|main.exe), NOT a 'lib' prefix. x86_64 runs natively under WSL / the ubuntu leg; arm64 under qemu-aarch64; pe64 (COFF .lib -> .exe) natively on the Windows leg (verified: fatlib.lib merges input.lib -> main.exe exit 42), build-clean + SkippedCrossHost elsewhere.
